content.question: Would structural conversion to a uniform comprehensive or 4-year-only sistema (without 6/8-year early selection) reduce between-school socioeconomic segregation and performance inequality measurably in the Hungarian context, or would free primary-school choice and other mechanisms sustain segregation at similar levels?
content.reduction_path: Analyze the degree to which segregation patterns in Hungarian primary schools (which lack selective tracking at entry) differ from secondary patterns; examine regional or municipality-level variation in secondary-school segregation correlated with historical presence/absence of early-selection tracks to estimate the partial contribution of 6/8-year systems to total segregation.
